item.title: Dozens rescued in Texas after torrential rain triggers flash flooding
item.description: More than a foot of rain has fallen since Monday, triggering dangerous flash flooding​ in central Texas.

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch new attacks and hurl threats as war intensifies
item.description: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Sons say they learned father was killed by ICE through video, news reports
item.description: Ronaldo Salgado said he learned his father, 52-year-old Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, had been shot by an ICE officer in Houston last week through a video posted online that depicted him "screaming" for help.

item.title: Tillis says he won't support Blanche unless he meets with Epstein survivors
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis said that Todd Blanche must meet with survivors of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's crimes before he'll vote to advance his nomination for attorney general out of the Senate Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Husband of Code Pink founder being probed under federal foreign agent and tax laws
item.description: Neville Roy Singham, who lives in Shanghai, China, is a major financial backer of a New York City-based nonprofit called the People's Forum, a left-leaning organization advocating for causes affecting the working class.

item.title: Sen. Welch presses former AG John Ashcroft on the dynamic between president, attorney general
item.description: Democratic Sen. Peter Welch questioned former Attorney General John Ashcroft during Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing on the relationship dynamics between a president and their attorney general.

item.title: White House teleprompter operator allegedly made nearly $100k betting on Trump's speeches
item.description: A White House teleprompter operator allegedly made nearly $100,000 betting on President Trump's speeches on the prediction market Kalshi, CBS News has confirmed. CBS News' Zak Hudak reports.

item.title: Mayor to homeowner losing sleep over data center plant: "Consider selling"
item.description: Noise from a Mississippi plant powering Elon Musk's xAI data centers in the area is causing migraines, ringing ears and sleepless nights, residents say.

item.title: Mississippi homeowners decry noisy data center plant: "I feel like I'm being tortured"
item.description: Residents of Southaven, Mississippi, are sounding off about a data center plant powering Elon Musk's xAI data centers in the area, likening the constant noise to being tortured.

item.title: Coast Guard suspends search for 3 missing boaters in San Francisco Bay
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard announced Wednesday that the search for three boaters missing after their vessel sank in San Francisco Bay will be suspended at sunset.

item.title: U.S. designates cartel on Texas border as foreign terrorist organization
item.description: The Juárez Cartel and Los Viagras joined six other Mexican criminal organizations that the U.S. considers terrorist groups.

item.title: Climate extremes fuel explosive wildfires in Canada and western U.S.
item.description: One climatologist said "a perfect storm" of climate extremes primed the western U.S. for one of its worst fire seasons in a decade. Meanwhile, Canadian wildfire smoke fills the air.

item.title: H.R. McMaster on the U.S.-Iran feud over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: H.R. McMaster, a CBS News contributor and President Trump's former national security adviser in his first term, joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings to discuss the current situation in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S.-Iran feud ramps up.

item.title: Fired DOJ lawyer attorney accuses Blanche of enforcing Trump's "pointless vendettas"
item.description: Former Justice Department pardon attorney Elizabeth Oyer accused Todd Blanche of unethical and sycophantic behavior during his confirmation hearing for attorney general. "The priority of this DOJ is protecting powerful men, even when it comes at the expense of vulnerable women," Oyer said, condemning Blanche's handling of the Epstein files. "These are not partisan concerns ... please do not degrade our justice system further by promoting Mr. Blanche."

item.title: Why the Trump administration is looking at Cuba military options
item.description: Senior defense officials are examining potential military options in Cuba, CBS News has learned. CBS News contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

item.title: Sons of Mexican man fatally shot by ICE describe grief and guilt over father's death
item.description: The sons of a Mexican man who was shot and killed by an ICE agent said they found out about the shooting through a video posted online. "It was a blur, really, that morning, because I really thought that he would be okay," one of his sons recalled to CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez.

item.title: Foreclosures surged 21% in the first half of 2026, new data shows
item.description: The state with the biggest jump in foreclosure activity was Idaho, where filings increased 59% compared to the same time last year.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: U.S. ramps up strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran overnight as tensions between the two countries continue to escalate. Iran responded with attacks on U.S. bases and facilities in the Middle East. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata has more.

item.title: Mom of murdered woman gives emotional testimony at Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing
item.description: Antioch, Illinois, mother Jennifer Bos voiced her support for Todd Blanche at his confirmation hearing to become attorney general. Bos advocates for stricter immigration policy after her daughter was allegedly murdered by an illegal immigrant.

item.title: Cape Verde goalie talks about team's inspiring World Cup run
item.description: Cape Verde's goalie Josimar José Évora Dias, known as Vozinha, talks with "CBS Mornings" about the team's inspiring run in the World Cup. The tiny country was playing in the tournament for the first time. Vozinha describes his unexpected rise to fame, challenges to becoming a professional player and his future in soccer.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Trump's DNI pick Jay Clayton refuses to answer on who won the 2020 election
item.description: President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, refused to answer who won the 2020 presidential election during a confirmation hearing. Meanwhile, Mr. Trump is expected to make a primetime speech Thursday night. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Zelenskyy's move to oust popular defense chief draws major backlash
item.description: Ukrainians are demonstrating in Kyiv and senior figures announcing their resignations over President Volodymyr Zelenksyy's move to oust his popular defense chief.

item.title: The Dish: Recipe: Try pickle-brined chicken salad for a summer favorite
item.description: In the series "The Dish: Recipe," Janice Carte, of Tiny Spoon Chef, shows how to make the light summertime recipe, pickle-brined chicken salad.

item.title: Trump to allege Chinese meddling in U.S. elections in primetime speech, sources say
item.description: Part of President Trump's speech Thursday night is expected to touch on previously unreported alleged Chinese meddling in U.S. elections, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Heavy smoke from Canadian wildfires impacts air quality from Minnesota to New York
item.description: Millions of people in the U.S. are under air quality alerts across parts of the Midwest and Northeast due to heavy smoke burning from dozens of wildfires primarily in Ontario, Canada. Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: "Jurassic Park" star Sam Neill's cause of death revealed
item.description: The actor's agent said he was providing more information following news reports "which contain inaccuracies and outright falsehoods."

item.title: State board: Musk may have broken election law by offering $1M checks to voters
item.description: The Wisconsin Elections Commission found billionaire Elon Musk may have violated the state's election bribery law by offering $1 million checks to voters during an election last year.

item.title: U.S. hits dozens of Iranian targets in retaliatory strikes after ship attacks
item.description: With peace talks on hold, the U.S. said it launched a "series of powerful strikes" on Iran in response to Iranian attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
